name: Lewis, Arthur B.
major: Postgraduates

name: Reece, O. Bickerstaff
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS; BACHELOR OF LAWS; College of Liberal Arts
description: If "Bick" has ever stopped singing, it was to laugh—but even his laugh is musical. He speaks of "his freshman year" as some people refer to their childhood, but still, popularity grows with years.

name: Barber, Harold Robert
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS; College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee, 1
description: Harold has done in three years what most men do in four—get a literary degree and make a host of college friends who will stand firm in years to come. A fine student, a gifted leader, and a popular man, he leaves Ole Miss with the best of records behind him.

name: Biles, James David Jr.
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS; College of Liberal Arts

name: Bowles, James Daniel
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: Jim has always been a jolly good fellow, the life of the gang, and one of "nature's noblemen." The cares and troubles of this life rest easily on his broad shoulders. This cheerful knocker of the blues will be hard to replace.

name: Breazeale, James L.
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: Breazeale is a quiet, industrious student, who, with his friendly greeting and pleasant manner, has won his way back into the hearts of the Ole Miss students after an absence of three years. He is destined to become one of Mississippi's foremost educators.

name: Broyles, Anne
major: College of Liberal Arts

name: Churchwell, Sam Breland
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: He is one of the perpetual students. He has not, however, been here every year since 1916. The philosophy of "live and let live" is his policy. He is a true friend, good student, and an outstanding character.

name: Carnathan, Sarah Margaret
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: She has lived up to her motto. She has gained the friendship of everyone, for whether it rains or whether the sun shines, she is always in the same good humor.

name: Corliss, John Johnson
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: He is a Grenada product. He is excellent in scholarship, faithful in friendship, upright in character, and liked by all who know him.

name: Crain, Sollie M.
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Sollie's the type of fellow generally known as "an all-round man." That little wrinkle in his forehead isn't a frown; he is only wondering just how to do his best. He usually does it, too.

name: Wesson, Monroe Crocker
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Formerly a student at Mississippi College, Crocker came to the university two years ago. So diligently has he applied himself to his work that on the campus the name "Crocker" has become almost a synonym for "knowledge."

name: Lewis, W. Cutrer
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: "Cooter," as he is known to all, is a prince in every sense of the word. He has a well-rounded character, and a personality that is forceful, and yet not overbearing. His aggressiveness in sports has won for him a courted place with the Ole Miss cagers.

name: George, W. Davis
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: Davis is a mature man of experience. He has learned the lesson that work honestly done never fails to be rewarded, and, as a result, he "works" and "thinks," and consequently "loafs" and "bulls" in no wise.

name: Dewey, Denton
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: A hard worker, an industrious student, and a man with unusual latent ability, Dewey is destined to win much fame as a pedagogue par excellence. In his years with us, Dewey has made many friends who wish him the heights of success in his dispensing of knowledge.

name: DeRouen, Julia Hazel
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: "Frenchie" comes to us from sunny Louisiana, with an adorable accent, big blue eyes, a stock of saucy French songs, and a great kindliness and generosity of nature, which includes a tolerance for all things. Here's wishing her "Bonne chance! Bon voyage!"

name: Fairley, Mildred Darrington
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: "Petite" Mildred is one of the many girls who have come from Belhaven and made good at Ole Miss. Little, sweet, and likeable, those who have known her will never forget her.

name: Friedman, Rosalie
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
description: Staunch and true, gentle and dignified, capable and studious, Rosalie has won during her four years at Ole Miss the affection and admiration of all who have known her.

name: Fuqua, Henry Hooker
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
description: Hooker possesses one of the most likeable personalities on the campus. His character is unimpeachable, unusually strong. He is a good musician, a fine dancer, and an excellent student.

name: Griffin, Reuben William
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: A true disciple of the forensic art; an ex-pedagogue; a rare personality; a man who commands the admiration of all who know him.

name: Godbold, Elizabeth Willard
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Cool, quiet, and reserved, no one has ever seen her excited. She meets life with a calm indifference and disposes of it with a gentle smile. She is an example of the "Survival of the fittest," having sailed through a four-year classical course, including the extras, with flying colors—and lived thereafter to tell the tale.

name: Hargis, Annie Clare
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Annie Clare is a girl of high ideals, combined with natural sweetness, strong mentality, and charm. Ole Miss is indeed proud to bestow upon Annie Clare the senior cap—the stamp and seal of her approval.

name: Herring, Joe Willie
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: Most everyone who has taken history knows Joe Willie—a little too serious—haunter of the library, though not a member of the "Club." A faithful "race horse"—his is a steady pace.

name: Hathorn, Olive Lucille
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
description: Although Lucille is quiet and seldom makes a fuss outside the classroom, her record shows that she is seldom silent within the classroom. Altogether, she has proved to be one of the best students that Ole Miss has ever turned out from within her portals.

name: Hyde, Gilbert Franklin
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: Gilbert comes to Ole Miss from Chickasaw College. Though he has been at Ole Miss only two years, he has made a place in the hearts of many students. He has allied himself with the good forces of the campus. May success award his efforts.

name: Hunt, Melville
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: Jolly, good-looking, and popular, everybody likes her, and she will succeed in anything she undertakes. "Mel" has an attentive and brilliant mind, easily capable of accomplishing much without undue exertion.

name: Jackson, Marjorie Walthall
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: Marjorie may sometimes be nonchalant, sometimes clever, sometimes sarcastic, but never stupid. She is one of the few who finish in three years. When you see her so often with her skates, however, you wonder how such a record is possible.

name: Jackson, Miriam Winter
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Legislative Council, Student Government for Women, '22, '25
description: Miriam came back to us after a two-years' absence. She is brainy and practical, and there are few things she cannot do; she thinks much, does much, and expresses much in a few words.

name: Johnson, Joseph
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: Joe came to Ole Miss with the idea of getting an education, and he did it. Deeply interested in his studies, he has taken small part in student activities, but it doesn't take prominence to be popular.

name: Jones, P. Z., Jr.
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
description: A friend you'll like is P. Z. He is slow to anger, agreeable in disposition, and friendly to everyone; his smile will be missed from the campus when he is gone.

name: Jones, Girault Mcarthur
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Phi Sigma, 1, 2, 3, 4; Censor, 2; Chaplain, 4; President, 4; Doorkeeper, 4
description: In "Jug" are found the qualities that make for greatness among college men. A good student and an honored man among his classmates, he leaves Oxford with many close personal friends. For loyalty to his friends, devotion to his duty, and love for Ole Miss, he is without a peer.

name: Jones, David William
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee
description: Besides devouring the "cinder path," Dave Willie has made good in the curriculum. Persevering to the utmost, he has that never-say-die spirit that is always sure to win out in any undertaking.

name: Kimmons, Elmira
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Elmira is a true friend, a courageous worker, and an executive person. She is loved, respected, and admired by a host of friends. Living in Oxford, Elmira has taken little part in student activities, but wherever she has taken part, she has proven her worth.

name: Kimbrough, Annie Walne
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Ole Miss is indeed indebted to Blue Mountain College for Annie Walne, for she has brightened our campus this year by her ready smile, studious mind, and attractive personality.

name: Lumpkin, Ben Gray
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Ben's familiar "time to close" will be missed next year by all our library patrons. Ben came to us from the city of "Cheviots," and during his four-year stay has made friends of everybody on the campus. All wish the very best of luck to "Prof. Lumpkin."

name: Lemasson, Viola Emma
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Viola is an excellent student and a hard worker. She does not know the word "procrastination" exists, and "duty" is her middle name.

name: Lyon, Elijah Wilson
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Phi Sigma, 1, 2, 3, 4; Reporter, 1; Vice-President, 2; President, 3; Critic and Anniversarian, 4
Y. M. C. A. Cabinet, 2, 3, 4; Vice-President, 3; President, 4
Member Ole Miss Board of Control, 4
description: Wilson's that kind of a fellow that falls into the class of "worth-while collegians." As "Big Blonde Brute" at the "Coop," as an outstanding leader among boys, and as Rhodes Scholar-Elect over the state, he has hosts of friends.

name: Miller, Russell Hale
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: An extraordinary talent is hidden in the quiet, unassuming Russell. With unselfish devotion he has done much for the betterment of student activities. He finishes his college career with a host of friends.

name: Milden, Gertrude
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Gertrude is her father's daughter and, therefore, brilliant. She has taken an active part in all campus activities, and she has acquitted herself creditably in all of them.

name: Walter, E. Mcdaniel
major: Bachelor of Arts
description: Because of his staying in town, he is not so well known as some. But those who know him and can claim his friendship are, indeed, fortunate. "Mac" is always ready and willing to help a good cause along.

name: David, Mcdowell
major: Bachelor of Arts
description: Dave has won a place in the hearts of all who know him for his sterling qualities as a true Southern gentleman. He possesses all of the essential characteristics demanded of a successful man—character, personality, and courage.

name: Margaret, Irene Mclean
major: Bachelor of Arts
description: Margaret went to Agnes Scott, and then decided to come to a real "he-man" school. She has a failing for A's, but her tendency to be a real student does not keep her from taking part in every wholesome activity of college life.

name: Wiley, Clifford Newman
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: "Parson" Newman will never be a "cardinal," but he has his "orders" already. No, W. C. is not "the man who rides alone."

name: Ross, Thomas Trotter
major: College of Liberal Arts

name: Smallwood, Sarah Katherine
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Kate has been a basketball star and a conscientious student. No one who has seen her spectacular guarding or who has been in her classes will forget the indomitable will she possessed, and the splendid strength of her character. She has her fate in her own hands. May she do wondrous things with it!

name: Smith, Marge
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Not many people have been fortunate enough to really know Marge, for she is studious and reserved; but those who have been thrown into close contact with her declare her sterling qualities and her true friendship.

name: Smith, Joe Lee
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts

name: Selby, Frank Smith
major: Bachelor of Science, College of Liberal Arts
description: Although Selby was forced to drop out of school for a year, he returns to graduate with his class. His college career has been marked by loyalty to Ole Miss, hard study, and devotion to the best college ideals.

name: Stevens, Ethel Josephine
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: From the time that "Joe" became an Ole Miss co-ed, she has shown marked ability on the basketball court, and has guarded the Ole Miss goal from foreign invasion for two years. In student activities she has likewise shown herself ready to take a prominent part.

name: Stokes, Rebecca
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: Rebecca has a rule which applies to both basketball and studies: Never, never miss the goal you shoot at. And she hasn't missed either goal! She has made herself a real Ole Miss co-ed in the short space of one year—that speaks for itself!

name: Thames, James DeLoach, Jr.
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Ole Miss Representative M. I. O. A., 3
description: On the stage and off, Jimmie is perfectly at home. With his pleasant personality and oratorical powers, he is sure to find a ready welcome wherever he settles.

name: Tartt, Elnathan Jr.
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: The striking thing about Ned is that he continues to be an active leader in many organizations and a conscientious and untiring student without sacrificing one whit his ability to hold enthralled the hearts of those whom he idolizes, the Daughters of the South.

name: Vardaman, Elizabeth
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
description: She is a happy-go-lucky and yet a conscientious student. Elizabeth is one of the jolliest and finest girls who ever attended Ole Miss. On the basketball team she was one of the stars.

name: Wallace, James David
major: Bachelor of Arts, College of Liberal Arts
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee, 3
description: James has the distinction of being our only member from Kemper County. During his two years at Ole Miss he has gained for himself a host of friends. When a problem of physics must be solved, ask James—he knows. We dare not prophesy what he may yet discover.

name: Wiley, Lois Etoy
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts
description: One would imagine Lois to be reserved, but one has to be with her a very short time to find hidden beneath that quiet, a spring of joy always bubbling over. In her work she is rated as one of the best students at the University.

name: Wilson, Frances Helen
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, College of Liberal Arts
description: Fanny's friendly manner and amiable personality makes her liked by all. She has many friends on the campus who wish for her the greatest success in life.

name: Wright, David Monroe
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, College of Liberal Arts

name: Allen, Thomas Dowe Jr.
major: School of Law
description: No matter where the wheels of Fate cast T. D., he is sure to succeed, for he is loyal and trustworthy. The legal profession should be proud to welcome into its midst a man of such high character and ability.

name: Brandon, Eugene Haughton
motto: "Judge" is one of the most loyal adherents Ole Miss has ever had. He has stood always for better things at Ole Miss. We may have many students at this institution, but never one with a higher sense of honor and integrity than has "Judge."
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS, School of Law
clubs: Blackstone Club, 1, 2, 3, 4; Secretary-Treasurer, 3; President Senior Law Class, 4
Y. M. C. A., 1, 2, 3, 4; Promotion Committee, 2; Cabinet, 3, 4; Executive Committee, A. S. B., 4

name: Boone, Richard M.
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS, School of Law
clubs: A. E. F., 2, 3, 4; Vice-President Senior Law Class, 4
description: Back from the battle against the Hun came Richard to battle against "precedent" and "decision." "Dick" has taken opportunity by the forelock, and while learning law has also attended diligently the "School of Politics."

name: Briggs, Charles Edward
motto: "Dig's" place as an admired idol in the hearts of the Ole Miss fans will ever rest secure.
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS, School of Law
description: "Dig" has undoubtedly received more cheers from the Ole Miss grandstands than any other athlete in years. For three years the ace of our pitching staff, and football manager for one.

name: Broadway, Lonnie Jackson
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law
description: A boy of great worthiness—worthiness in a "Broadway!" Lonnie bears no nickname, but, if one were needed, "Bonnie" would suit best. Always cheerful, always bright, even though hardships come, his smile never falters.

name: Clarke, Eugene Singleton
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law
description: "Gene," the witty, the shrewd, the keen, the merry, the likeable, is sure to win much success, as well as cause much merriment in his future role as barrister for various and sundry personages at law.

name: Denton, Partee L.
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law
description: Partee came to us from Vanderbilt last year. A good student, a steady worker, and a winning personality, he should some day grace the bench and add another to our distinguished roll.

name: Gober, Oscar P.
major: School of Law

name: Henderson, Arthur Sewall
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law
description: He is always cheerful. He never allows his work to interfere with his college career, although he is an "A" student. The success of the Ole Miss quartet depends greatly upon his baritone voice.

name: Hewes, Gaston Henderson
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law

name: Kennedy, William Ross
major: School of Law

name: Patton, Moody C.
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law
description: Moody can do most anything and do it well. He is seeking law as a profession rather than as a trade, and we predict for him a successful career.

name: Rosenthal, Simon
major: Bachelor of Laws, School of Law

name: Stennett, Irvin William
major: School of Law

name: Windham, Esker Kearley
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS
description: During his three-year sojourn at Millsaps, garnered all the honor that institution affords. Sighing for new worlds to conquer, he pitched camp at Ole Miss. Having learned all the Law and still sighing, he went to the legislature to make some more. He has made a reservation for his tent on Fame's eternal camping ground.

name: Abernethy, Thomas Gerstle
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS, LL.B., Cumberland, '24
description: Though Abby has been at Ole Miss for this year only, his droll wit and constant good humor have secured him a host of friends. Eupora must be a wonderful place, for, according to Ab, nothing seen nor heard of since the world began is new to that illustrious city.

name: Phillips, Henry Abright
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS; BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING; School of Engineering
description: Phil is an honor man from whom Ole Miss is expecting great things. He is never satisfied with ordinary results, and therefore does all things excellently. An exceedingly brilliant fellow who believes that doing is better than saying.

name: Brewer, William Clark
motto: "Shorty," beloved of Fortune, his ready wit makes him a prime favorite. In every field, be it classroom or lover's lane, he is a man of no mean ability.
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING; School of Engineering
clubs: Vice-President, Student Chapter American Society of Civil Engineers, 4

name: Boatner, Edmund B.
motto: When the roll of good fellows is called, "Flop" will answer "Present." "Flop" is the kind of a fellow that is bound to succeed. He has a level head, a fine personality, and is the owner of such a genial smile as is significant of amiability in its truest sense.
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING; School of Engineering
clubs: Treasurer, Student Chapter American Society of Civil Engineers, 4

name: Hoff, Arthur Stanley
motto: Ole Miss will always be indebted to Illinois for "Art." His work on the athletic field and in the council hall has helped us in a great way. In addition to these attributes, he has a likeable smile, a fine personality, and a heart of gold.
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING; School of Engineering

name: Ritchey, Hume
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING, School of Engineering
description: The engineering profession is indeed fortunate in having Ritchey enter its ranks. He has been a good student and is well liked by all those who know him. The sky is the limit of success with such as he.

name: Oakley, Earl
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING, School of Engineering
clubs: Student Chapter, American Society of Civil Engineers, 3, 4
description: His famous smile and cheerful greeting endear him to one and all. We can do no better than to wish him great success in the pursuit of his profession.

name: Overstreet, Munsey Gordon
major: School of Engineering

name: Tomlinson, Samuel Alexander Jr.
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
clubs: Student Chapter American Society of Civil Engineers, 3, 4
description: S. A. is one of those fellows that everyone knows and who knows everyone. He has taken his work, his friends, his activities, and the campus with his smile. Take good fellowship, leadership, and a genial smile, and that's Sam.

name: Thompson, George Walton
major: School of Engineering

name: Applewhite, Norman Elliot
major: School of Medicine

name: Cox, John
major: School of Medicine

name: Hand, William Fitzgerald
major: B.S., M.D., School of Medicine
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee, 2
description: In his four years at Ole Miss, Pete has missed none of his opportunities. Although one of the hardest students on the campus, he has not buried himself from his many friends. His influence has always been cast for the best in college life.

name: Holland, Clell G.
major: School of Medicine

name: Frank, Lo Presto
major: B.S., M.D., School of Medicine
description: The "baboon" comes to us from Greenwich Village. Such is his versatility that with equal facility he can "mow" his way from an examination room unobserved, argue the merits of Darwin's theory, and actually fool Dr. Crider into believing that he is awake during a physiology quiz.

name: Ellis, Columbus Moore
major: M.D., School of Medicine
description: Moore is known to his many friends as a good, big-hearted scout. His excellent personality and his lasting determination will carry him to the height of his chosen profession.

name: Lonnie, Moseley
major: School of Medicine

name: O'ferrall, R. Carter
major: School of Medicine

name: Strange, Jack Ezell
major: School of Medicine

name: Walker, Richard Henry Jr.
major: B.S., M.D., School of Medicine
description: "A typical college man" describes Dick better than any other phrase which we might assign to him. He has taken part in all student activities of any nature. In time he will no doubt metamorphose into a distinguished doctor.

name: Zeller, Raymond Bryant
major: M.D., School of Medicine
description: Raymond is a jolly fellow having as his one hobby the making of "The Scream" a success. Whatever you may think of his methods, he is an outstanding example of what aggressiveness and determination can do in campus life.

name: Martin, William Halbert
major: B.S., M.D., School of Medicine
description: Again we say that you cannot keep a "good man down," for A. & M. could not hold a man of Halbert's broadness. He is a true and sincere pal, whose memory his classmates will long cherish.

name: Leggett, Carter Wiley
major: Bachelor of Science in Commerce, School of Commerce
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee
description: A splendid student and a likeable man is Carter. As a student assistant in Spanish, he is efficient, and therefore successful. Carter is quiet in disposition, conscientious in his work, and persistent in all his efforts.

name: Izard, Billie Pam
major: Bachelor of Science in Commerce, School of Commerce
description: In Billie we find that rare combination of a genuine student and stellar athlete. In the Commerce School, Izard's scholarship won for him several assistantships. As a basketball player he has taken his place with Mississippi's greatest.

name: Tays, Henry Clyde
major: Bachelor of Science in Commerce, School of Commerce
description: Tays is like the reputed Nib—the more you know him, the better you like him. He travels daily at a gait that he can sustain and is free from spasmodic spells; he has demonstrated a commendable consistency.

name: Smith, Wayne Butler
major: Bachelor of Science in Commerce, School of Commerce
description: Wayne is a hard fighter who never gives up; he has won in football; it seems that he has won in the co-ed tryouts, and we are sure that he will win in business life. Wayne will be greatly missed on both the athletic field and the co-ed stamping ground.

name: Akin, William Edwin Jr.
major: School of Pharmacy

name: Price, Cain
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: Although Price has been with us only one year, he has been found to possess sterling qualities of manhood. His smile is engaging, and his personality exceedingly attractive. He cannot but succeed in his chosen profession.

name: Alden, F. Cranford
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, B.S., School of Pharmacy
description: Cranford spent two years at Millsaps College before he came to Ole Miss. His first year here was taken up in the pursuit of his B.S. degree, but last year he undertook the study of pharmacy. He has shown himself to have many of the qualities which make for success in any profession.

name: Roy, Andrew Dean
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy

name: Farr, Edward Stanley
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Farr is one of the outstanding students of the Pharmacy School; he is found taking an active part in everything that goes on around him. We expect him to return to his home when he finishes and secure a position in the Bluff City.

name: Flowers, James Clair
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Quiet in his nature, diligent in his work, and persistent in his purpose, Flowers is one who believes that "doing" is better than "talking about doing."

name: Flowers, John Kelvin
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: John is exemplary of the old axiom that "Still waters run deep." He thinks much, does much, and expresses much in few words. Ole Miss loses a good student in John.

name: Flowers, William Glenn
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: "Red" should have the grand prize for being the most quiet student at Ole Miss. He runs on the assumption that "not words, but deeds show what's in the man." Watch him succeed.

name: Gates, Henry Howell
major: School of Pharmacy

name: Albert, Haley
major: School of Pharmacy

name: Lee, William Curtis
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: Lee was one of the stars of the freshman basketball team of '24, and has continued his record by starring on the varsity this year. You know, he hails from a community called Woolmarket, north of Biloxi, where they play basketball all the year as a major and minor sport, both.

name: Lorick, Lee
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: Lee is a good sport. It was not until his second year that he discovered the advantages of pharmacy. Lee finishes this year and goes forth into the world as a designated "pill roller" for the public.

name: Marron, Claude L.
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy

name: McMillion, Tom Henderson
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: Tommie came to us his first year with the intention of making a first-class pharmacist. In all his work he has made good, and his desire to reach the top is being granted by the earnest labor on his part. We wish him all the success in the pharmaceutical work.

name: Potter, Frederick Wesley
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: Fred comes to us from the hills of South Mississippi—down where the wind whistles through the southern pines—from a little sawmill town. However, you would never know that fact from an observation of Fred himself. He has the ability to carry through anything that he undertakes.

name: Rhodes, Samuel Sedwick
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: In the short time that Sam has been with us, few have really gained his friendship, but those few have been richly repaid for their efforts. He is the type of fellow that you have to know in order that you may appreciate him fully.

name: Robbins, William Edgar
major: GRADUATE IN PHARMACY, School of Pharmacy
description: W. E., or "Bill," as he is better known, is a man of sterling character and admirable personality. He is one whom we all respect and are glad to call a friend, for there is no truer friend than he. With his industry and stickability, he is sure of success in any field.

name: Sanders, Cody Alexander
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: C. A. is a quiet, unassuming, dependable fellow who can be counted on to make his mark after he leaves Ole Miss. Throughout his student life he has ever shown himself to be an industrious worker, a loyal friend, a devoted student, and above all, a gentleman of the highest type.

name: Sessions, John King
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Full of grit, humor, and confidence—that's "King John"—and one of the best all-round fellows we have on the campus. If you are fortunate enough to win his friendship there is nothing too small or too great for him to do for you.

name: Vance, Oliver G.
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Vance desires to give the public the best of his services as a pharmacist. From the work that he has done while at the University, we know that he will be able to give them his best.

name: Waldrup, T. C.
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Waldrup came here ready for a fight, and has been putting up that fight that only a winner can put up. After he has left us, may he win that success that he so fairly fought for.

name: Rozell, Rowan Walker
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Oh, yes! He is a good pharmacist, but at that he shouldn't waste his looks in a drug store. The movies would seem more appropriate for him, but since he prefers pharmacy, we feel certain that he will make a success of it.

name: Tom, Zollicofer Whitehead
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Tom, a relative of the Zollicoffer Browns of former days at Ole Miss, is continuing to hold up the family standard with no uncertain success. This is only made possible by Tom's becomingly quiet personality.

name: Henry, A. Wilkinson
major: Graduate in Pharmacy, School of Pharmacy
description: Henry, or "Pie-Face," as he is better known, entered Ole Miss in the fall of '22, dropped out the following year to enjoy a peaceful existence in a lazy little town of Neshoba County, found such an existence had lost its appeal for him, and now has returned to complete the task he started. Now we suppose that he will start out somewhere to make his mark as a druggist.
